learning_outcomes.description: Critically review current practice and use an evidence-informed approach to plan a strategically aligned enhancement initiative
Implement and evaluate the effectiveness of an enhancement initiative.
Collaborate with members of a community of practice in teaching and learning at UQ
Critically reflect on personal learning across course and impact on professional practice and personal development.

assessments.task_description:
The best way to enhance your teaching is to start with a solid plan. This task will help you to review your current practice, identify a strategic change you can make this semester to enhance your approaches and environments and to support and guide learners. You will identify the supports you need to put in place to succeed.

You will complete a template:

• drawing together information you already have including your teaching philosophy and context, 

• identifying people you need to work with and tasks you need to complete,

• strategically planning your change and the support you need to get there.

The template will be provided through Blackboard (Learn.UQ) and we will work through this template in Workshop 1 identifying support available in this course and beyond to implement your enhancement plan.

Feedback on this task will be discussed in Workshop 2.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: As this assessment is graded on a pass/fail basis, late submissions cannot be accepted for penalty marks. If you anticipate any difficulties meeting the due date, please contact the Course Coordinator as early as possible to discuss your options. Extensions may be granted in line with UQ’s assessment policy where appropriate.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date: Negotiated selection of date & time in weeks commencing 31/08/26 or 05/10/26.
assessments.task_description:
Pitch your change and get feedback through this presentation and dialogue This task challenges you to refine your plan for how you will implement your change to 4-minute presentation. You will then engage in a 4-minute dialogue, responding to questions on rationale, methodology, and potential impact to securely demonstrate your work. 

You may be completing this assessment before you implement your change, alongside your change or soon after your change.

A PowerPoint template will be provided to support your presentation on Blackboard (Learn.UQ). The presentations and dialogues will be held on campus with two assessors and small group of your peers.

This oral presentation and dialogue is to be completed in-person. This task will be recorded to support assessment activities by the teaching team and review as required by HEA accreditation and UQ Assessment Procedures.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Negotiated selection of date & time in weeks commencing 31/08/26 or 5/10/26
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: As this assessment is graded on a pass/fail basis, late submissions cannot be accepted for penalty marks. Extensions may be granted in line with UQ’s assessment policy where appropriate.

assessments.task_description: You will be required to submit a completed template signed off by a suitably experienced teacher who observers you engaging with your students around the change you are implementing through this course.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: As this assessment is graded on a pass/fail basis, late submissions cannot be accepted for penalty marks. If you anticipate any difficulties meeting the due date, please contact the Course Coordinator as early as possible to discuss your options. Extensions may be granted in line with UQ’s assessment policy where appropriate.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: In this culminating task, you’ll develop a case of your practice and reflect on your professional learning journey through the Teaching in Practice program. Drawing on your Enhancement Proposal, Change Presentation, and Teaching Observation, you will demonstrate how your practice has evolved, grounded in the HEA Professional Standards Framework (PSF) and ready to use in your Annual Performance & Development (APD). This reflective task not only showcases your growth as an educator at UQ but also provides authenticated evidence of your effectiveness.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 15(20 Jul - 08 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Self Directed Learning

Throughout this course you will have time dedicated to engage with literature, resources and your teaching. This time will support you planning, reflecting on and implementing your changed teaching practice. You will be responsible for your independent learning to achieve the outcomes of this course.


Learning outcomes: L01, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Self Directed Learning Throughout this course you will have time dedicated to engage with literature, resources and your teaching. This time will support you planning, reflecting on and implementing your changed teaching practice. You will be responsible for your independent learning to achieve the outcomes of this course.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop 1: Course Introduction

This workshop will introduce the course, providing an overview of assessment tasks, activities, and concepts. In this workshop you will meet the course staff and make significant progress towards completing assessment 1. This workshop will be delivered in face-to-face mode only and is compulsory for continuing the course.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Workshop 1: Course Introduction This workshop will introduce the course, providing an overview of assessment tasks, activities, and concepts. In this workshop you will meet the course staff and make significant progress towards completing assessment 1. This workshop will be delivered in face-to-face mode only and is compulsory for continuing the course.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 4 To Week 14(10 Aug - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Specialisation Modules

Throughout this course you will select at least one specialisation module. Specialisation modules involve synchronous and independent activities. In these modules, you will work with experts in the specialisation area and work with tools or resources to help identify opportunities for enhancement, guide reflection on your practice, and implement changes.


Learning outcomes: L01, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Specialisation Modules Throughout this course you will select at least one specialisation module. Specialisation modules involve synchronous and independent activities. In these modules, you will work with experts in the specialisation area and work with tools or resources to help identify opportunities for enhancement, guide reflection on your practice, and implement changes.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop 2: Feedback, Presentation and Dialogue, and your Peer Learning Community

This workshop will explore the feedback from Assignment 1, prepare you for Assessment 2 and introduce you to your Peer Learning Community (PLC).

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_title: Workshop 2: Feedback, Presentation and Dialogue, and your Peer Learning Community

learning_activities.topic:
Presentation and Dialogue (Assessment 2) - Option 1

Pitch your change and get feedback through this presentation and dialogue.

Learning outcomes: L01, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Presentation and Dialogue (Assessment 2) - Option 1 Pitch your change and get feedback through this presentation and dialogue.

learning_activities.topic:
Peer Learning Community #1

Supportive peer to peer social learning groups that meet regularly to support and challenge to enable exploration of ideas and gather feedback on change project and enhancements in a safe, supportive environment. There are two timetabled peer learning community (PLC) activities and you are welcome to connect with your PLC throughout the semester. 

Learning outcomes: L01, L03

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop 3: Writing your Case Study and Reflection

This workshop will support your writing about your teaching in preparation for Assessment 4, annual planning and development and other contexts. Through this workshop we will be reflecting on your experiences through this course aligned to the Professional Standards Framework. We will also be developing our case studies and preparing you for future enhancements to your teaching practice. 

Learning outcomes: L02, L04
